meaning: The position after the ninth; one of ten equal parts.
example: This is the tenth iteration of the software.
example2: We are on the tenth release of the project.
example3: The error was located in the tenth module.
Explanation: The word 'tenth' is an ordinal number. In programming, remember that most languages use zero-based indexing, meaning the 'tenth' item is actually at index 9. This is a common area of confusion for new developers. 'Tenth' is used to describe sequences, versions, or list positions in documentation or project planning. Always double-check your index numbering to avoid off-by-one errors.
usageTip: Use 'tenth' for ordering; remember zero-based indexing in code.
commonMistake: Treating the 'tenth' item as index 10 (it's index 9).

meaning: The number 10 in a sequence or one of ten equal parts
example: This is the tenth version of the software.
example3: One tenth of the files are corrupted.
Explanation: The word 'tenth' is an ordinal number. It represents the position 10 in a sequence or a fraction representing one of ten parts ($1/10$). It is used in software versioning (e.g., the 10th iteration), counting items, or specifying parts of a whole. It follows standard English numbering rules. There is no verb or adjective form beyond the word itself (which acts as both). It is basic but necessary vocabulary.
usageTip: Use 'tenth' for ordering or splitting things into ten parts.
